school crossing supervisor Listen Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Print Content components School crossing supervisors have a much-valued role in Boroondara. On an average school day in Boroondara, they help 9,266 pedestrians to cross the street while 74,426 motor vehicles use our roads during school crossing times. We are always looking for school crossing supervisors. If you are friendly, reliable and flexible, this role is for you. What does a school crossing supervisor do? This paid position gives you the opportunity to get involved in your community and improve road safety for children, people with a disability and the general public who use our 114 school crossings. School crossing supervisors: • work part-time Monday to Friday, from 8 am to 9 am and from 3 pm to 4 pm, during the school term • play an important role in educating children with ‘Stop, Look and Listen’ instructions • are responsible for reporting any road hazards or potential risks • help to encourage active and independent travel. About you We are looking for people aged 18 and older who: • can attend shifts at short notice • have their own reliable transport • are able to work across several locations • can complete the pre-employment medical and police checks. You will have access to a full training program before starting your role. Download the position description School Crossing Supervisor PD 144.04 KB [PDF] 23 November 2022 How to apply Learn more about this rewarding role by reading the position description above. Submit an online application through our Careers website . Attend an interview – meet with one of our Council Officers at Council, located at 8 Inglesby Road in Camberwell. Provide us with relevant employment documents – including your Working With Children Check, Police Check and evidence of your COVID-19 vaccination status. Complete a medical assessment. Apply to become a School Crossings Supervisor We are always recruiting new school crossing supervisors to help people to cross our roads safely at peak school times. More information Call (03) 9278 4774 or email [email protected] for more information. Careers at Boroondara Learn more about what it's like to work with us. Learn more Was this page helpful? closing_text: Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) Requisition ID 15080 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Local Laws Officer Join a dynamic, investigations team that supports each other to succeed Deliver meaningful outcomes to the community in safety and amenity $91,334 - $98,476 per annum (plus Super) Tool of trade vehicle provided (non-hybrid position) Join us in building a professional investigations team and help deliver a responsive, community-focused Local Laws service. In this role, you will contribute to a safe, liveable and well-maintained municipality while providing high-quality regulatory service to residents and stakeholders. About the role At City of Boroondara, our Local Laws directly contributes to our purpose of protecting the health, safety, amenity and assets of the municipality. Based within the large and busy department of Civic Services, the team works as part of five field-based teams and many others within the Urban Living Directorate. You will respond to reported breaches of the Amenity Local Law and the Environment Protection Act, using education, investigation and enforcement to achieve compliance. The role involves managing a varied caseload, gathering evidence, preparing clear records and working within established procedures to resolve matters fairly and effectively. The position includes investigating issues such as illegally dumped rubbish under the EPA, unsightly properties, overhanging vegetation, illegally advertising and the occupation of Council land, while providing courteous and timely service to community members. From time to time, you will also support the Animal Management team with park patrols, and work with external stakeholders including Victoria Police, the Magistrates’ Court and Council prosecutors. Hours are allocated over a 16-week roster (42-hours per week) and rotating weekend work is required every 4 weeks with penalty rates that convert into an annualised salary. A fully maintained vehicle is provided as a ‘tool of trade’ to perform necessary duties and commute to and from work. This is a non-hybrid role as our ‘office’ is in the community, every day and our success comes from working together with other officers, as a team! About you We are looking for someone who brings sound judgement, a strong customer focus and a collaborative approach. You will be supported to build your knowledge and succeed in the role. You will have experience delivering customer service in a complex regulatory environment or another fast-paced setting, with the ability to communicate clearly, build rapport and respond professionally in a range of situations under pressure. Experience in areas such as retail, hospitality, community services or other enforcement role can provide a strong foundation for success in this role. You will be able to investigate issues methodically, collect information accurately and prepare clear notes and reports. Knowledge of compliance, local laws or related legislation will be well regarded, as will the ability to interpret policy and apply it in practical situations. If you are still developing in these areas, a willingness to learn will be equally valued. Success in this role also requires respectful negotiation skills, sound problem-solving and judgement skills, de-escalating interpersonal situations with members of the community, and the capacity to work across the community each day, including walking for extended periods and assisting with animal handling when required. A qualification in enforcement, local laws, justice or a related field is desirable, but not essential. Want to know more? Please refer to the Position description What sets us apart? An investment in your ongoing professional and personal development You will be part of a team that makes a real difference. Working at Boroondara means contributing to creating positive change in the world. Join us in a role where your work matters, and you contribute to meaningful outcomes. A supportive and friendly workplace culture that values diversity, collaboration and continuous improvement. We offer many staff benefits such as flexible working arrangements, study support, wellbeing programs, gym and pool memberships Employment requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check. All City of Boroondara employees must be fully vaccinated. Appointment of the successful candidate will be subject to provision of evidence of vaccination status or medical exemption prior to commencement. How to Apply Follow these steps, carefully: Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply ’ button by Sunday 5 July 2026 11:59pm . Ensure you address your cover letter to Natasha Curmi and answer the following questions: What about the Local Laws Officer role motivates you? Why is this the next logical step in your career? All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. No calls or applications from agencies please. For any confidential discussion please contact Natasha Curmi, Team Leader Compliance (during business hours) on 0459 854 662 . We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. advertised_salary_text: $93,155 + Superannuation Ongoing, full-time About Boroondara Boroondara is continuously evolving
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Transfer Station - Driver Attendant Operator (15134) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Transfer Station - Driver Attendant Operator (15134) Requisition ID 15134 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Great location on Riversdale Rd, Camberwell Attractive remuneration, $93,155 + Superannuation Ongoing, full-time About Boroondara Boroondara is continuously evolving as part of our ongoing commitment to become a truly customer centric organisation. Through technology, innovation, collaboration and leadership, we are realigning how we work together to enhance the customer experience. An exciting opportunity has arisen to play a vital role supporting our Transforming Boroondara Program. About the role We are seeking a Transfer Station - Driver Attendant Operator to work in our Transfer Station and Yard Maintenance team, which is located at the Boroondara Waste and Recycling Centre operated by the City of Boroondara. The team is responsible for the receiving, organising and cartage of hard, household, recyclable and organic waste. Our team members enjoy variety of work in a very busy operational work site which is open 7 days a week. If you have experience in the Waste industry - whether from private industry or councils, or you come from the building and earthmoving industries, we welcome your application. Key responsibilities of the role include: The morning is spent receiving council’s internal waste trucks and directing our drivers where to park for waste to be unloaded. In the afternoon the Transfer Station operates a public service, for the public to deposit their hard waste and recycling materials. This involves interacting with the public, traffic management and providing clear directions where to deposit waste such as organic matter, mattresses, tyres, steel, bricks etc. Throughout the day all waste is loaded onto trailers for cartage to landfill, sorting centres and mulching sites. All the Transfer Station and Yard Maintenance team members are trained in all tasks and are required to work on all duties , ranging from control room operation, yard maintenance and cleaning, delivery of bins and skips in accordance with pre-arranged programs, assist in after-hours emergencies and vehicle maintenance, internal Transfer Station traffic management. About you To be successful in this role you will bring: It is in your nature to be a productive team member and have a high work ethic. You will join a small and already cohesive team, and you have excellent interpersonal skills. You will use your OHS skills to ensure your work site is secure from hazards and is safe for you, your colleagues and the public, and promptly report hazards. At a minimum this role, requires a Heavy Combination (HC) Licence, and you must already be licensed when applying. If you recently received your HC Licence, we welcome your application regardless of the number of accumulated driving hours. If you have any one of the following certificates - backhoe, front end loader or forklift, this will be an advantage. If you don’t have these 3 certificates, training will be provided with training paid by the City of Boroondara. Additional information This is a Monday to Friday commitment. You will be required to work 1 Saturday in every 7 weeks. The following Friday will be a paid day off (RDO). Your ordinary hours of work are 42.5 hours a week, starting at 5am, 6am or 7am. Overtime will be paid only if you work more than 42.5 hours a week. The advertised salary is inclusive of all Public Holidays which are worked. Public Holidays not worked are Good Friday, Christmas Day and New Years Day and are paid at normal time. ‘Make up’ days are prior to or after these 3 Public Holidays and are on a Saturday only. Appropriate overtime will be paid on these 3 days. The AFL Grand Final Parade Day is worked and will be paid at the appropriate overtime rates. Why Join us? You will join a great work environment which offers: a great team culture and a fair and supportive manager active learning and development opportunities prime office location in Camberwell with cafes, retail and restaurants nearby generous parental leave and other leave entitlements wellbeing programs, free flu vaccinations, free skin checks Want to know more? Check out the Position Description Employment requirements To be eligible to secure a position, you must either be an Australian citizen, or If you have an Australian government issued visa, you must have Australian residence status (for tax purposes) that allows you to reside and work in Australia indefinitely. If you have a limited stay Visa which has an end date on your visa, you are not eligible to apply. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Check, a Driving History Report, a Medical Assessment and a Working with Children Check. How to apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11:59pm Sunday 28 June 2026. All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Bill Seremetis on 0447 307 880 during business hours. I f you wa nt to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our careers website boroondara.vic.gov.au/careers The City of Boroondara is committed to providing systems that are accessible to as many people as possible. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some positions may require the incumbent to hold a Working with Children Check. Working together We are currently undertaking an exciting project that will see a transformation in the way we work and the way we serve our customers. Implementation of the Transforming Boroondara Program will provide a customer centric model of service delivery that includes technological innovation, process improvement and business system renewal - we value your input and look forward to our journey ahead. #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the closing_text: Career Opportunities: Branch Lead (15122) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Branch Lead (15122) Requisition ID 15122 - Posted - Job Function (3) - Tenure Status (2) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Branch Lead Fantastic opportunity to take on a leadership role Permanent, Full time position (35 hours per week) Camberwell Library $101,985 - $110,964 per annum (Band 6), plus Superannuation Are you an enthusiastic, customer-focused leader ready to make a real impact in a vibrant community setting? We’re looking for a passionate and dynamic Branch Lead to help shape the future of library services at Camberwell Library. This is an exciting opportunity to lead a dedicated team, deliver contemporary library experiences, and contribute to a service that connects, inspires, and supports our community every day. A little bit about the role Our six libraries are thriving community spaces and in 2024/25 there were more than: 2 million loans 970,000 visits 38,000 program attendees 81,000 enquiries As Branch Lead, you’ll oversee the day-to-day operations of Camberwell Library, ensuring services continue to meet the diverse and evolving needs of the Boroondara community. This is a fast-paced and rewarding leadership role where you will: Deliver contemporary library services that support recreation, culture, education, and access to information Lead, support, and develop staff to foster a collaborative and high-performing team culture Build strong internal and external partnerships and community networks Contribute to strategic projects and continuous service improvements Champion community engagement and lifelong learning initiatives Support innovative digital and customer service experiences A little bit about you You’re an experienced people leader who is passionate about libraries, community-focused services and creating positive customer experiences. You will bring: Strong leadership and team management skills A collaborative and positive approach to staff development A commitment to innovation and continuous improvement Excellent relationship-building and community engagement skills Experience delivering high-quality customer services Library experience is highly desirable, but not essential Why join us? At Boroondara Library Service, you’ll be part of a service that makes a genuine difference in the community every day. You’ll work alongside passionate colleagues, contribute to meaningful programs and initiatives, and help create welcoming spaces where people can connect, learn, and grow. Employment Requirements Tertiary qualification in library and information science or relevant experience, is advantageous. National Police Record Check and valid Working with Children Clearance required. How to Apply If you’re ready to lead a busy, community-focused library and help shape exceptional customer experiences, we’d love to hear from you. To express interest in this opportunity please do so online via the ‘ Apply’ button by 11:59pm Tuesday 30 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. Please indicate which role/s you are applying for in your cover letter. For any further information please refer to the position description , or contact Aileen Carter during business hours on (03) 9278 4020 for a confidential discussion. If you want to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our Careers Website. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some applicants may be required to hold a Working with Children Clearance. Additionally, some applicants may also be required to undergo a National Criminal History check and/or medical check as per the requirements of the role. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. With so much going on it’s a great time to join Boroondara! #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the title: Branch Lead Fantastic opportunity to take on a leadership role

advertised_salary_text: $ 101,985 per annum (pro rata) (Band 6A) plus Superannuation An exciting opportunity has arisen for
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Early Years Planning and Development Officer (15101) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Early Years Planning and Development Officer (15101) Requisition ID 15101 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Early Years Planning and Development Officer • Part time permanent position (34.8hrs per week) • Fantastic opportunity to join our Early and Middle Years team! • $ 101,985 per annum (pro rata) (Band 6A) plus Superannuation An exciting opportunity has arisen for an experienced Early Years Planning and Development Officer to join our team and contribute to improving outcomes for children and families in the community. A little bit more about the role You will be responsible for supporting the planning, development and implementation of early years initiatives, programs and services across the municipality, including overseeing Boroondara’s kindergarten Central Registration and Enrolment Scheme (CRES). The role will contribute to strategic planning, stakeholder engagement and delivery of early years infrastructure projects, ensuring services and facilities meet the needs of a growing and diverse community. You will also work collaboratively across Council and with external partners to support integrated service delivery and promote inclusive participation across early childhood services. What are we looking for? To be successful in this role you will bring demonstrated knowledge of early years frameworks, policies and current sector trends, along with experience working with community groups and service providers. You will have excellent communication and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work effectively with a diverse range of stakeholders. Strong organisational skills, attention to detail and a commitment to delivering high-quality outcomes for the community are essential. Qualifications and Employment Requirements • Degree qualification in early childhood, community development, social planning or a related field • Experience working within the early years sector, preferably in a local government setting • Understanding of relevant legislation, policy and frameworks (e.g. Early Years Learning Framework) • Current Victorian Driver’s Licence To gain a real insight into this fantastic opportunity we recommend referring to the position description or contacting the hiring manager, Leanne Parnis, Early and Middle Years Lead , on 03 9278 4848 for a confidential discussion. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by the 11:59pm on Monday, 29 June 2026. All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. description_excerpt: Early Years Planning and Development Officer • Part time permanent position (34.8hrs per week) • Fantastic opportunity to join our Early and Middle Years team! • $ 101,985 per annum (pro rata) (Band 6A) plus Superannuation An exciting opportunity has arisen for an experienced Early Years Planning...

closing_text: Career Opportunities: Safer Communities Project Officer (15090) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Safer Communities Project Officer (15090) Requisition ID 15090 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Safer Communities Project Officer Play a key role in promoting community safety and fostering participation in community life. Part time, fixed term position for 4 months (23 hours per week). $84,770 to $98,205 (pro rata to be applied) per annum plus Superannuation Make a difference by coordinating and delivering a variety of projects and events with a focus on fostering participation in community life and promoting community safety. A little bit about the role Reporting to the Health Planning Team Leader, the Safer Communities Project Officer will be responsible for assisting with the delivery of a variety of projects and community events such as Community Safety Month (including the Emergency Family Fun Day and Community Safety Forum), International Women’s Day, 16 Days of Activism Against Gender Based Violence Campaign, and Homelessness Week. The role will also support delivery of projects in the health and wellbeing space, and the implementation of Council’s Homelessness Protocol, as required. This includes supporting the Safer Communities Planner (Homelessness) to coordinate Council’s approach to managing complex issues relating to health and safety issues that may arise at sites where people are sleeping rough in the municipality. A little bit about you You will have outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal, be personable and approachable and able to engage with a range of audiences including, external agencies and groups, Victorian Government departments, service providers, and the community. You will have a demonstrated understanding of health, wellbeing and safety issues impacting the community, for example family violence and homelessness. You will have experience in project development and the delivery of community events. You will also have comprehensive computer skills across the Microsoft Office range of applications, a willingness to work as part of a team and prepared to share experiences with other team members to improve overall knowledge. The successful incumbent will also have: post-secondary qualifications with relevant work experience a commitment to quality outcomes and efficient work practices, and excellent time management and organisation skills Employment Requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check and hold a valid Working with Children Clearance. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply’ button by 11:59pm Sunday 21 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. For any further information please refer to the position description , or contact Katherine Wright (Senior Coordinator Social Planning & Research) during business hours on 9278 4827 for a confidential discussion. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. advertised_salary_text: $71,312 - $76,789 Band 3(plus Superannuation) + 9 Day Fortnight The Role We’re looking for a Turf Maintenan
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Turf Maintenance Officer (15113) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Turf Maintenance Officer (15113) Requisition ID 15113 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Full time, permanent opportunity ( 2 Positions available) Based at Freeway Golf Course Depot - BALWYN NORTH $71,312 - $76,789 Band 3(plus Superannuation) + 9 Day Fortnight The Role We’re looking for a Turf Maintenance Officers(x2) to join our Environmental Sustainability and Open Spaces team. This role is responsible for the day-to-day care and maintenance of Boroondara’s 54 sportsgrounds. You’ll be based at the Freeway Golf Course Depot but will work across a range of sites to support the delivery of high-quality turf services for our community . Key Responsibilities In this role, you’ll carry out a wide variety of turf maintenance tasks including mowing, herbicide and fertiliser application, irrigation repair, safety audits, and contributing to reconstruction works. You'll also support seasonal renovations, undertake tractor operations, and assist with wicket preparation where required. Your daily work will help ensure that our sportsgrounds are safe, attractive, and ready for use by local clubs and residents alike. You’ll be part of a passionate and skilled turf management team, working collaboratively and rotating across sites as needed. You'll participate in toolbox meetings, report hazards, and contribute to a strong safety-first culture. Accurate recordkeeping and a commitment to completing tasks on time and to a high standard are essential . About you We’re looking for someone who holds a Certificate III in Sports Turf Management, with demonstrated hands-on experience in the maintenance of sportsgrounds or similar turf surfaces. You’ll have a sound understanding of turf health, irrigation systems, and the safe use of turf-related machinery and chemicals. A current driver’s licence is required, and experience in wicket curation or working within local government would be a bonus. If you would like more information regarding this opportunity , please refer to our position description. Employment requirements • Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. • Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check. If you're passionate about turf care, enjoy working outdoors, and take pride in maintaining high-quality public spaces, we’d love to hear from you. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11.59pm, Tuesday 30 June 2026. All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Nick Marino, Coordinator Turf Management on 0425 341 966 during business hours or email nick.marino@boroondara.vic.gov.au We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. closing_text: Career Opportunities: Tree Crew Supervisor (15022) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Tree Crew Supervisor (15022) Requisition ID 15022 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Full time role, to lead and organise Tree Crew operations, located in Kew $86,620 - $100,054 plus super and allowances 9 day fortnight, plus tool of trade vehicle The Opportunity We are seeking a dedicated and experienced Tree Crew Supervisor to join our Open Space and Trees team. This is a critical role in leading the daily operations of Council’s Tree Crew, ensuring the delivery of safe, high-quality arboricultural services. T Key Responsibilities Arrange responses to urgent requests Manage a small crew to provide a positive and engaged team culture, that operations safely and in compliance with relevant legislation Manage the maintenance and replacement of Tree Crew plant and equipment Arrange contractors, issue work orders Provide a high level of customer service, and detailed record keeping. We are seeking someone: Experienced in tree maintenance, pruning and removals. Strong leadership skills; able to motivate and support staff. Confident managing high workloads and frequent customer contact. Effective communicator who builds good working relationships. Organised, safety‐focused and able to manage competing priorities. Comfortable interpreting legislation and using digital systems. For more information, please refer to the position description Employment Requirements Applicants must hold unrestricted working rights in Australia, and those progressing through the selection process will need to complete a National Police Record Check, a Working with Children Check and relevant pre-employment medical assessments. How to apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply Now’ button by Friday 26th June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Darren Bowtell, Senior Arborist, Reactive Maintenance on (03) 9278 4444 during business hours or email commencing $61,425 pa (pro-rata) / $31.09 per hour + Super + Allowances Casual - $38.86 per hour (incl. loading) + Super + Allowances Closing Date: Applications are accepted at all times. We are seeking skilled and experienced professional care workers who love supporting people to live independently in the community. We currently provide services in Mount Alexander Shire and Central Goldfields Shire. This is your opportunity to bring your passion and skills to be part of a professional and dedicated team providing high quality in-home support to frail older people and people with disabilities in our community. The role provides tailored services to help clients live healthy, independent and active lives through assistance with daily tasks and activities. If you have any queries or would like to discuss the role, the key contact is: Sarah Newstead In Home Support Coordinator (03) 5471 1845 Please note these positions require staff to be triple vaccinated (2 doses plus a booster). To apply, please provide a cover letter (no more than 2 pages) outlining why you are interested and the best candidate for this role, and your current resume. Mount Alexander Shire Council is committed to child safety. We have zero tolerance of child abuse. Our robust human resources, recruitment and vetting practices are strictly adhered to during the application and interviewing process. Applicants should be aware that we conduct working with children, police and reference checks to ensure that we are recruiting the right people. Apply now Email this job Back to top Acknowledgement of Country Mount Alexander Shire Council acknowledges that the Traditional Custodians of this land, the Dja Dja Wurrung and Taungurung peoples, proudly survive. We acknowledge their continued practise of custom and their close cultural, spiritual, physical, social, historical and economic relationship with the land and waters that make up their Country, which includes Mount Alexander Shire. closing_text: PAGE 1 Position Description IMMUNISATION NURSE Created 15/05/2026 Alpine Shire Council’s Values - ICARE Innovation | Collaboration | Accountability | Respect | Empowerment Our shared values and behaviours are integral to how we go about our work, interact with each other and our community. Child Safety Commitment at Alpine Shire Council Alpine Shire Council is committed to being a child safe organisation. Every child and young person has the right to feel safe. All our employees have a shared responsibility to protect children and young people from harm and abuse. Alpine Shire Council have established policies, procedures and training to achieve these commitments. Diversity & Inclusivity at Alpine Shire Council Alpine Shire Council is an Equal Opportunity employer. We value people of all abilities, people from culturally and/or linguistically diverse backgrounds and First Nations peoples. We are committed to upholding a safe, inclusive and respectful workplace that values the contribution of all. POSITION OVERVIEW The immunisations team work to promote and maintain high standards of public health and wellbeing within our community, with a focus on disease and illness prevention. You will provide an efficient, responsive and responsible service. You will be required to promote the Alpine Shire Council as a customer-focused organisation at all times. You will undertake key responsibilities in a professional, efficient and timely manner, and contribute to Council’s aim of being a high performing organisation. You will need to be passionate and multi-skilled and able to consistently deliver at high standard operational services in a tidy, organised, caring and safe environment. You will contribute towards the public health agenda by improving health outcomes for our children, young people and our community. CLASSIFICATION AND RELATIONSHIPS Classification: Nurses (ANF –Victorian Local Government Award 2015) varied by the Alpine Shire Council Enterprise Agreement 2023. Reports to: Maternal and Child Health Nurse Coordinator. Supervises: Nil KEY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Accountability and extent of authority • Accountable to direct supervisor and manager. • Duties performed within Council policies and procedures. • Duties to be performed within specified timeframes. • Authority to liaise with internal and external customers to ensure accurate and up to date information is maintained in a timely manner. Immunisations • Administer vaccines at immunisation sessions as required and in accordance with the National Immunisation Program requirements and National Immunisations Framework for Health Professionals. • Assist to ensure accurate recording of all relevant client information. • Ensure the confidentiality of client information is maintained as required. • Ensure the safe keeping of materials and equipment. • Equipment and resources are managed effectively to reduce waste and promote cost efficiency. • Provide assistance with determining an efficient annual schedule of immunisations for Alpine Shire, including: immunisations for 0-5 year old children, the secondary school program, Council staff and older eligible adult influenza vaccine sessions. • Maintain a constant drive towards service improvement. Other duties • Ensure all processing and tasks undertaken are efficient. • Display a punctual, reliable and positive approach to work. • Continually review and improve work methods associated with this role. • Undertake other duties as directed by the manager or supervisor. • In addition to the responsibilities described in this document, all employees must comply with the Alpine Shire Council’s Code of Conduct and Corporate Policies. Judgement and decision making • Guidance is always available from immediate supervisor or manager • The particular tasks to be performed may involve selection from a • limited range of existing techniques, systems, equipment, methods or processes in a defined range of recurring work situations. Management skills • Ability to manage your time and plan your day to achieve specific and set objectives. Interpersonal skills • Good oral and written communication skills with customers, other employees and members of the public and in the resolution of minor problems. • Display a punctual, reliable and positive approach to work • Ability to project a positive image in communicating with both internal and external customers at all times EXPERTISE Essential Qualifications • Victorian driver licence. • Current and valid nursing registration with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation (AHPRA). • Authorised Immunisation provider. • Working with Children Check. Experience • Previous experience in delivering immunisations and good working knowledge about the National Immunisation Program. Specialist Skills and Knowledge • Understanding of the role including relevant frameworks and guidelines. • Undertaking any personal development as required of registrations and/or as changes are required of the National Immunisation Program. • Sound written and verbal communication skills. • Keen interest and knowledge of vaccine preventable diseases of public health significance and immunisation. • Capacity to prioritise, organise individual work plan and to work semi- autonomously. • Capacity to communicate confidently and independently with senior health professionals and members of the public. HEALTH, SAFETY AND RISK DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Nothing is more important than the health and safety of yourself and those around you. You must: • Perform all tasks in a manner that does not place at risk the health and safety of any person. • Seek assistance when unsure of how to perform a task. • Ensure you observe, understand and comply with all Occupational Health and Safety Legislation, Regulations, Codes of Practice, Policies, Procedures and directives. • Regularly inspect your work environment for hazards. • Immediately report all workplace incidents, accidents, near-misses and hazards to a supervisor. • Support the return-to-work plan for any injured worker when returning to work. • Council Employees do not smoke in any workplace or when engaged in work related activities. Smoking is an issue for both Council and employees as it impacts on both the work and personal life of the smoker and other employees and in some cases the reputation of the Council. PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS Daily work will be performed in an outdoor environment, as such: • You will be exposed to those conditions normally encountered in an indoor Environment. • Physical demands are moderate to strenuous, consisting of the ability to sit, stand, stoop, reach, bend, climb, lift and pull using safe manual handling practices. • • You must be able to see and hear in the normal range, with or without correction, and communicate well both verbally and in written form, and must be able to be understood. • You must have the stamina to work long hours, if assigned, and must be willing to work an irregular schedule, which may include weekends, holidays, evenings, and/or varying shifts. • The work environment may include uneven surfaces and up and down stairs.

school crossing supervisor Listen Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Print Content components School crossing supervisors have a much-valued role in Boroondara. On an average school day in Boroondara, they help 9,266 pedestrians to cross the street while 74,426 motor vehicles use our roads during school crossing times. We are always looking for school crossing supervisors. If you are friendly, reliable and flexible, this role is for you. What does a school crossing supervisor do? This paid position gives you the opportunity to get involved in your community and improve road safety for children, people with a disability and the general public who use our 114 school crossings. School crossing supervisors: • work part-time Monday to Friday, from 8 am to 9 am and from 3 pm to 4 pm, during the school term • play an important role in educating children with ‘Stop, Look and Listen’ instructions • are responsible for reporting any road hazards or potential risks • help to encourage active and independent travel. About you We are looking for people aged 18 and older who: • can attend shifts at short notice • have their own reliable transport • are able to work across several locations • can complete the pre-employment medical and police checks. You will have access to a full training program before starting your role. Download the position description School Crossing Supervisor PD 144.04 KB [PDF] 23 November 2022 How to apply Learn more about this rewarding role by reading the position description above. Submit an online application through our Careers website . Attend an interview – meet with one of our Council Officers at Council, located at 8 Inglesby Road in Camberwell. Provide us with relevant employment documents – including your Working With Children Check, Police Check and evidence of your COVID-19 vaccination status. Complete a medical assessment. Apply to become a School Crossings Supervisor We are always recruiting new school crossing supervisors to help people to cross our roads safely at peak school times. More information Call (03) 9278 4774 or email [email protected] for more information. Careers at Boroondara Learn more about what it's like to work with us. Learn more Was this page helpful? closing_text: Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) Requisition ID 15080 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Local Laws Officer Join a dynamic, investigations team that supports each other to succeed Deliver meaningful outcomes to the community in safety and amenity $91,334 - $98,476 per annum (plus Super) Tool of trade vehicle provided (non-hybrid position) Join us in building a professional investigations team and help deliver a responsive, community-focused Local Laws service. In this role, you will contribute to a safe, liveable and well-maintained municipality while providing high-quality regulatory service to residents and stakeholders. About the role At City of Boroondara, our Local Laws directly contributes to our purpose of protecting the health, safety, amenity and assets of the municipality. Based within the large and busy department of Civic Services, the team works as part of five field-based teams and many others within the Urban Living Directorate. You will respond to reported breaches of the Amenity Local Law and the Environment Protection Act, using education, investigation and enforcement to achieve compliance. The role involves managing a varied caseload, gathering evidence, preparing clear records and working within established procedures to resolve matters fairly and effectively. The position includes investigating issues such as illegally dumped rubbish under the EPA, unsightly properties, overhanging vegetation, illegally advertising and the occupation of Council land, while providing courteous and timely service to community members. From time to time, you will also support the Animal Management team with park patrols, and work with external stakeholders including Victoria Police, the Magistrates’ Court and Council prosecutors. Hours are allocated over a 16-week roster (42-hours per week) and rotating weekend work is required every 4 weeks with penalty rates that convert into an annualised salary. A fully maintained vehicle is provided as a ‘tool of trade’ to perform necessary duties and commute to and from work. This is a non-hybrid role as our ‘office’ is in the community, every day and our success comes from working together with other officers, as a team! About you We are looking for someone who brings sound judgement, a strong customer focus and a collaborative approach. You will be supported to build your knowledge and succeed in the role. You will have experience delivering customer service in a complex regulatory environment or another fast-paced setting, with the ability to communicate clearly, build rapport and respond professionally in a range of situations under pressure. Experience in areas such as retail, hospitality, community services or other enforcement role can provide a strong foundation for success in this role. You will be able to investigate issues methodically, collect information accurately and prepare clear notes and reports. Knowledge of compliance, local laws or related legislation will be well regarded, as will the ability to interpret policy and apply it in practical situations. If you are still developing in these areas, a willingness to learn will be equally valued. Success in this role also requires respectful negotiation skills, sound problem-solving and judgement skills, de-escalating interpersonal situations with members of the community, and the capacity to work across the community each day, including walking for extended periods and assisting with animal handling when required. A qualification in enforcement, local laws, justice or a related field is desirable, but not essential. Want to know more? Please refer to the Position description What sets us apart? An investment in your ongoing professional and personal development You will be part of a team that makes a real difference. Working at Boroondara means contributing to creating positive change in the world. Join us in a role where your work matters, and you contribute to meaningful outcomes. A supportive and friendly workplace culture that values diversity, collaboration and continuous improvement. We offer many staff benefits such as flexible working arrangements, study support, wellbeing programs, gym and pool memberships Employment requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check. All City of Boroondara employees must be fully vaccinated. Appointment of the successful candidate will be subject to provision of evidence of vaccination status or medical exemption prior to commencement. How to Apply Follow these steps, carefully: Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply ’ button by Sunday 5 July 2026 11:59pm . Ensure you address your cover letter to Natasha Curmi and answer the following questions: What about the Local Laws Officer role motivates you? Why is this the next logical step in your career? All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. No calls or applications from agencies please. For any confidential discussion please contact Natasha Curmi, Team Leader Compliance (during business hours) on 0459 854 662 . We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. advertised_salary_text: $93,155 + Superannuation Ongoing, full-time About Boroondara Boroondara is continuously evolving
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Transfer Station - Driver Attendant Operator (15134) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Transfer Station - Driver Attendant Operator (15134) Requisition ID 15134 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Great location on Riversdale Rd, Camberwell Attractive remuneration, $93,155 + Superannuation Ongoing, full-time About Boroondara Boroondara is continuously evolving as part of our ongoing commitment to become a truly customer centric organisation. Through technology, innovation, collaboration and leadership, we are realigning how we work together to enhance the customer experience. An exciting opportunity has arisen to play a vital role supporting our Transforming Boroondara Program. About the role We are seeking a Transfer Station - Driver Attendant Operator to work in our Transfer Station and Yard Maintenance team, which is located at the Boroondara Waste and Recycling Centre operated by the City of Boroondara. The team is responsible for the receiving, organising and cartage of hard, household, recyclable and organic waste. Our team members enjoy variety of work in a very busy operational work site which is open 7 days a week. If you have experience in the Waste industry - whether from private industry or councils, or you come from the building and earthmoving industries, we welcome your application. Key responsibilities of the role include: The morning is spent receiving council’s internal waste trucks and directing our drivers where to park for waste to be unloaded. In the afternoon the Transfer Station operates a public service, for the public to deposit their hard waste and recycling materials. This involves interacting with the public, traffic management and providing clear directions where to deposit waste such as organic matter, mattresses, tyres, steel, bricks etc. Throughout the day all waste is loaded onto trailers for cartage to landfill, sorting centres and mulching sites. All the Transfer Station and Yard Maintenance team members are trained in all tasks and are required to work on all duties , ranging from control room operation, yard maintenance and cleaning, delivery of bins and skips in accordance with pre-arranged programs, assist in after-hours emergencies and vehicle maintenance, internal Transfer Station traffic management. About you To be successful in this role you will bring: It is in your nature to be a productive team member and have a high work ethic. You will join a small and already cohesive team, and you have excellent interpersonal skills. You will use your OHS skills to ensure your work site is secure from hazards and is safe for you, your colleagues and the public, and promptly report hazards. At a minimum this role, requires a Heavy Combination (HC) Licence, and you must already be licensed when applying. If you recently received your HC Licence, we welcome your application regardless of the number of accumulated driving hours. If you have any one of the following certificates - backhoe, front end loader or forklift, this will be an advantage. If you don’t have these 3 certificates, training will be provided with training paid by the City of Boroondara. Additional information This is a Monday to Friday commitment. You will be required to work 1 Saturday in every 7 weeks. The following Friday will be a paid day off (RDO). Your ordinary hours of work are 42.5 hours a week, starting at 5am, 6am or 7am. Overtime will be paid only if you work more than 42.5 hours a week. The advertised salary is inclusive of all Public Holidays which are worked. Public Holidays not worked are Good Friday, Christmas Day and New Years Day and are paid at normal time. ‘Make up’ days are prior to or after these 3 Public Holidays and are on a Saturday only. Appropriate overtime will be paid on these 3 days. The AFL Grand Final Parade Day is worked and will be paid at the appropriate overtime rates. Why Join us? You will join a great work environment which offers: a great team culture and a fair and supportive manager active learning and development opportunities prime office location in Camberwell with cafes, retail and restaurants nearby generous parental leave and other leave entitlements wellbeing programs, free flu vaccinations, free skin checks Want to know more? Check out the Position Description Employment requirements To be eligible to secure a position, you must either be an Australian citizen, or If you have an Australian government issued visa, you must have Australian residence status (for tax purposes) that allows you to reside and work in Australia indefinitely. If you have a limited stay Visa which has an end date on your visa, you are not eligible to apply. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Check, a Driving History Report, a Medical Assessment and a Working with Children Check. How to apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11:59pm Sunday 28 June 2026. All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Bill Seremetis on 0447 307 880 during business hours. I f you wa nt to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our careers website boroondara.vic.gov.au/careers The City of Boroondara is committed to providing systems that are accessible to as many people as possible. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some positions may require the incumbent to hold a Working with Children Check. Working together We are currently undertaking an exciting project that will see a transformation in the way we work and the way we serve our customers. Implementation of the Transforming Boroondara Program will provide a customer centric model of service delivery that includes technological innovation, process improvement and business system renewal - we value your input and look forward to our journey ahead. #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the advertised_salary_text: $101,985 - $110,964 per annum (Band 6), plus Superannuation Are you an enthusiastic, customer-focused leader
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Branch Lead (15122) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Branch Lead (15122) Requisition ID 15122 - Posted - Job Function (3) - Tenure Status (2) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Branch Lead Fantastic opportunity to take on a leadership role Permanent, Full time position (35 hours per week) Camberwell Library $101,985 - $110,964 per annum (Band 6), plus Superannuation Are you an enthusiastic, customer-focused leader ready to make a real impact in a vibrant community setting? We’re looking for a passionate and dynamic Branch Lead to help shape the future of library services at Camberwell Library. This is an exciting opportunity to lead a dedicated team, deliver contemporary library experiences, and contribute to a service that connects, inspires, and supports our community every day. A little bit about the role Our six libraries are thriving community spaces and in 2024/25 there were more than: 2 million loans 970,000 visits 38,000 program attendees 81,000 enquiries As Branch Lead, you’ll oversee the day-to-day operations of Camberwell Library, ensuring services continue to meet the diverse and evolving needs of the Boroondara community. This is a fast-paced and rewarding leadership role where you will: Deliver contemporary library services that support recreation, culture, education, and access to information Lead, support, and develop staff to foster a collaborative and high-performing team culture Build strong internal and external partnerships and community networks Contribute to strategic projects and continuous service improvements Champion community engagement and lifelong learning initiatives Support innovative digital and customer service experiences A little bit about you You’re an experienced people leader who is passionate about libraries, community-focused services and creating positive customer experiences. You will bring: Strong leadership and team management skills A collaborative and positive approach to staff development A commitment to innovation and continuous improvement Excellent relationship-building and community engagement skills Experience delivering high-quality customer services Library experience is highly desirable, but not essential Why join us? At Boroondara Library Service, you’ll be part of a service that makes a genuine difference in the community every day. You’ll work alongside passionate colleagues, contribute to meaningful programs and initiatives, and help create welcoming spaces where people can connect, learn, and grow. Employment Requirements Tertiary qualification in library and information science or relevant experience, is advantageous. National Police Record Check and valid Working with Children Clearance required. How to Apply If you’re ready to lead a busy, community-focused library and help shape exceptional customer experiences, we’d love to hear from you. To express interest in this opportunity please do so online via the ‘ Apply’ button by 11:59pm Tuesday 30 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. Please indicate which role/s you are applying for in your cover letter. For any further information please refer to the position description , or contact Aileen Carter during business hours on (03) 9278 4020 for a confidential discussion. If you want to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our Careers Website. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some applicants may be required to hold a Working with Children Clearance. Additionally, some applicants may also be required to undergo a National Criminal History check and/or medical check as per the requirements of the role. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. title: Branch Lead Fantastic opportunity to take on a leadership role

advertised_salary_text: $ 101,985 per annum (pro rata) (Band 6A) plus Superannuation An exciting opportunity has arisen for
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Early Years Planning and Development Officer (15101) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Early Years Planning and Development Officer (15101) Requisition ID 15101 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Early Years Planning and Development Officer • Part time permanent position (34.8hrs per week) • Fantastic opportunity to join our Early and Middle Years team! • $ 101,985 per annum (pro rata) (Band 6A) plus Superannuation An exciting opportunity has arisen for an experienced Early Years Planning and Development Officer to join our team and contribute to improving outcomes for children and families in the community. A little bit more about the role You will be responsible for supporting the planning, development and implementation of early years initiatives, programs and services across the municipality, including overseeing Boroondara’s kindergarten Central Registration and Enrolment Scheme (CRES). The role will contribute to strategic planning, stakeholder engagement and delivery of early years infrastructure projects, ensuring services and facilities meet the needs of a growing and diverse community. You will also work collaboratively across Council and with external partners to support integrated service delivery and promote inclusive participation across early childhood services. What are we looking for? To be successful in this role you will bring demonstrated knowledge of early years frameworks, policies and current sector trends, along with experience working with community groups and service providers. You will have excellent communication and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work effectively with a diverse range of stakeholders. Strong organisational skills, attention to detail and a commitment to delivering high-quality outcomes for the community are essential. Qualifications and Employment Requirements • Degree qualification in early childhood, community development, social planning or a related field • Experience working within the early years sector, preferably in a local government setting • Understanding of relevant legislation, policy and frameworks (e.g. Early Years Learning Framework) • Current Victorian Driver’s Licence To gain a real insight into this fantastic opportunity we recommend referring to the position description or contacting the hiring manager, Leanne Parnis, Early and Middle Years Lead , on 03 9278 4848 for a confidential discussion. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by the 11:59pm on Monday, 29 June 2026. All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. With so much going on it’s a great time to join Boroondara! #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the closing_text: Career Opportunities: Safer Communities Project Officer (15090) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Safer Communities Project Officer (15090) Requisition ID 15090 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Safer Communities Project Officer Play a key role in promoting community safety and fostering participation in community life. Part time, fixed term position for 4 months (23 hours per week). $84,770 to $98,205 (pro rata to be applied) per annum plus Superannuation Make a difference by coordinating and delivering a variety of projects and events with a focus on fostering participation in community life and promoting community safety. A little bit about the role Reporting to the Health Planning Team Leader, the Safer Communities Project Officer will be responsible for assisting with the delivery of a variety of projects and community events such as Community Safety Month (including the Emergency Family Fun Day and Community Safety Forum), International Women’s Day, 16 Days of Activism Against Gender Based Violence Campaign, and Homelessness Week. The role will also support delivery of projects in the health and wellbeing space, and the implementation of Council’s Homelessness Protocol, as required. This includes supporting the Safer Communities Planner (Homelessness) to coordinate Council’s approach to managing complex issues relating to health and safety issues that may arise at sites where people are sleeping rough in the municipality. A little bit about you You will have outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal, be personable and approachable and able to engage with a range of audiences including, external agencies and groups, Victorian Government departments, service providers, and the community. You will have a demonstrated understanding of health, wellbeing and safety issues impacting the community, for example family violence and homelessness. You will have experience in project development and the delivery of community events. You will also have comprehensive computer skills across the Microsoft Office range of applications, a willingness to work as part of a team and prepared to share experiences with other team members to improve overall knowledge. The successful incumbent will also have: post-secondary qualifications with relevant work experience a commitment to quality outcomes and efficient work practices, and excellent time management and organisation skills Employment Requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check and hold a valid Working with Children Clearance. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply’ button by 11:59pm Sunday 21 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. For any further information please refer to the position description , or contact Katherine Wright (Senior Coordinator Social Planning & Research) during business hours on 9278 4827 for a confidential discussion. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state.
